name: Nero Wolfe's Roast Quail Veronique
recipeIngredient: 12 Slices Fritz's bread (good homemade will do)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Wash and wipe the quail dry. Rub the insides with salt and pepper. Stuff each bird with the wild rice mixed with a little of the melted butter. Truss with butcher's cord.
3. Put the quail in a shallow roasting pan, brush with butter, and roast for 5 minutes at 450°F. Lower the heat to 325°F and roast for 20 minutes more, basting with additional butter.
4. Remove from the pan and keep warm on a platter.
5. Deglaze the pan with the wine and veal bouillon and bring to a boil. Lower the heat and add the grapes, simmering for 5 minutes. Correct the seasonings.
6. Fry the bread in a little butter and cut into triangles. Arrange the toast on a serving platter and cover with julienned slices of ham. Place the quail on top and spoon some of the sauce over them. Serve the rest of the sauce in a sauceboat.
